news.title: Samsung Confirms Launch of AI Smart Glasses in 2026
news.summary: Samsung has announced plans to launch AI smart glasses in 2026, featuring a 12MP camera, 155mAh battery, and Qualcomm’s AR1 chip. These glasses aim to provide a multimodal AI experience, combining voice, vision, and sensors for real-time interaction.
news.content: Samsung’s EVP of Mobile Experiences, Seong Cho, confirmed the company's plans to launch wearable devices, including AR glasses, in 2026 during the Q4 2025 earnings call. The new glasses are designed to work with AI assistants and sensors rather than full mixed-reality displays, reflecting Samsung’s strategy to expand its wearable ecosystem. Early reports suggest these will be lightweight and Samsung-branded, offering intuitive AI support for daily tasks.

news.title: Apple Plans to Launch AI Smart Glasses by Late 2026
news.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to release a pair of smart glasses equipped with a camera, microphone, and Siri integration in late 2026. The device is expected to offer various functions such as analyzing the environment, making calls, playing music, and providing real-time translation and navigation services.
news.content: According to Bloomberg, Apple plans to release smart glasses that will have the capability to execute voice commands through Siri. The device is seen as a direct competitor to Meta Ray-Ban smart glasses. While Apple is moving forward with the development of AI-powered smart glasses, they have decided to cancel the plans for the Apple Watch and Apple Watch Ultra models that were supposed to come with cameras in 2027.

news.title: Apple Testing Standalone Siri App for iOS 27
news.summary: Apple is reportedly testing a standalone Siri app for iOS 27 that works like a chatbot, with a new look and 'Ask Siri' button across apps.
news.content: The new standalone Siri app is expected to provide a more integrated and seamless experience for users, allowing for easier access to Siri's functionalities across the entire iOS ecosystem. This update signifies Apple's continuous efforts to enhance its AI capabilities and user interaction within its devices.

news.title: Hikvision to Showcase AI-Powered Industrial Solutions at Hannover Messe 2026
news.summary: Hikvision, a global leader in AIoT solutions, is set to participate in Hannover Messe 2026, demonstrating how AI-powered perception and intelligent analytics are transforming industrial operations.
news.content: Hikvision will exhibit at Hall 27-G84, showcasing its commitment to 'Shaping Industrial Digitalization with AIoT.' The company's focus will be on how AIoT solutions can revolutionize manufacturing and energy sectors through advanced perception and analytics technologies.

news.title: Vivo Halts Smart Glasses Project
news.summary: Vivo has halted its smart glasses project after months of joint development with partners including Goertek and ThunderSoft, due to lack of differentiation in a crowded market.
news.content: Executives at Vivo reportedly concluded that the product lacked differentiation in a market already crowded with AI-powered eyewear. This move highlights the broader challenges facing smart glasses, from hardware complexity to a thin software ecosystem.

news.title: Zephyr RTOS Reaches New Milestone with Enhanced Security Features
news.summary: The Zephyr project has announced a new release of its RTOS, focusing on enhanced security features and improved support for the latest hardware platforms.
news.content: The latest version of Zephyr RTOS includes significant updates to its security framework, providing developers with more robust tools to protect their embedded systems against potential threats. Additionally, the release adds support for new hardware platforms, expanding the range of applications for Zephyr in the IoT and embedded markets.

news.title: Google and Warby Parker Confirm 2026 Launch of AI Smart Glasses
news.summary: Google has confirmed that its first AI smart glasses, developed in collaboration with Warby Parker, will debut in 2026, marking a major expansion of its Android XR ecosystem.
news.content: The upcoming devices aim to blend hands-free assistance, real-time information access, and stylish, all-day wearability. The glasses are designed to integrate seamlessly into everyday routines, allowing users to ask questions about their surroundings, recall information, and communicate more efficiently through real-time translation features.

news.title: Atlassian to Lay Off 1,600 Employees to Boost Investments in AI
news.summary: Atlassian announced a workforce reduction of 10%, affecting 1,600 employees, to boost investments in AI and enterprise sales.
news.content: Following the news, shares rose by 2%. Co-founder Mike Cannon-Brookes acknowledged the difficult decision in a memo to employees. The company aims to self-fund investments for AI and enterprise sales, indicating a strategic shift towards AI technology and solutions.
